pr.title: Finish data match and linking of multiple Ness items
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Finished matching/linking work for several Ness item units by adding missing item data tables, correcting the old PK Flash naming from `pkflush`/`pkflushexplode` to `pkflash`/`pkflashexplode`, updating includes and item logic callbacks, and flipping the affected objects to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The decomp.dev bot reported +12020 linked-code bytes, +336 matched-data bytes, +552 linked-data bytes, and 41 new matches; the 35 broken matches shown are largely the old `itnesspkflush` unit names being replaced by `itnesspkflash` names.

key_files.role: Changed multiple Ness item object files from `NonMatching` to `Matching` and corrected build paths from `itnesspkflush*` to `itnesspkflash*`.
Renamed split units at the same address ranges from `itnesspkflush.c` and `itnesspkflushexplode.c` to `itnesspkflash.c` and `itnesspkflashexplode.c`.
Renamed PK Flash symbols, adjusted local/global scope on several functions/data labels, and replaced some placeholder global data names with compiler-style local `@NNN` labels.
Renamed from `itnesspkflush.c`, updated function prefixes to `itNesspkflash_*`, added `ItemStateTable it_803F6B70[]`, and kept short collision helpers static/local.
New replacement header for the old `itnesspkflush.h`, declaring PK Flash APIs and `extern ItemStateTable it_803F6B70[]`.
Renamed from `itnesspkflushexplode.c`, updated symbols to `itNessPKFlashExplode_*`, and added `ItemStateTable it_803F6F40[]`.
Updated item logic table entries and includes to call the renamed PK Flash and PK Flash Explode callbacks.
Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6BC8[]` for PK Thunder Ball.
Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6C08[]`, defined `s32 it_803F6C18[]`, and added the needed `it/forward.h` include.

what_changed: Corrected the item unit naming from `PK Flush` to `PK Flash` across split names, source filenames, headers, include sites, symbols, and item logic table callback names.
Added missing `ItemStateTable` definitions for several Ness items: `it_803F6B28`, `it_803F6B70`, `it_803F6BC8`, `it_803F6C08`, `it_803F6C68`, and `it_803F6F40`.

Added the PK Thunder Trail kind array definition `s32 it_803F6C18[] = { It_Kind_Ness_PKThunder1, ..., It_Kind_Ness_PKThunder4 }` in the C file and removed its old `ItemKind[]` extern declaration from the header.
Updated `it_2725.c` item logic tables to reference `itNesspkflash_Logic102_*` and `itNessPKFlashExplode_Logic103_*` instead of the old `PKFlush` names.
Changed Ness and Kirby fighter source includes to use `it/items/itnesspkflash.h`, reflecting the corrected item header.

Adjusted symbol metadata so static/no-op collision helpers such as `itNesspkflash_UnkMotion1_Coll`, `itNesspkflash_UnkMotion2_Coll`, `itNessPKFlashExplode_UnkMotion0_Coll`, and `itNesspkthunderball_UnkMotion0_Coll` are local rather than global.
Regenerated or corrected data symbol labels in `symbols.txt`, including local compiler labels like `@249`, `@333`, `@376`, `...data.0`, and `...bss.0` for matched data sections.
smart_moves: Handled the `pkflush` to `pkflash` correction as an all-at-once rename across build config, splits, symbols, headers, source, and call sites, avoiding duplicate aliases or stale includes.
Added concrete `ItemStateTable` data before flipping object files to `Matching`, which is the right order for data-match/linking work.
Kept callback pointer order explicit in the state tables, preserving the expected `Anim`, `Phys`, and `Coll` table layout for item state data.
Made tiny collision callbacks static/local where they are only table-internal, reducing unnecessary global symbol exposure and matching the local symbol scopes.

Accepted that the bot would show old `itnesspkflush` matches as broken because the split unit was renamed; the net result still improved linked code/data and created equivalent `itnesspkflash` matches.
Updated fighter code that only includes the item header, showing that item renames must propagate into Ness and Kirby copied-special consumers even when the main logic is in the item system.
decomp_lessons: For item objects, code matching and data matching are coupled: `ItemStateTable` arrays and item logic table references must match before the object can safely become `Matching`.
A semantically wrong unit name can distort progress reports; after renaming `PKFlush` to `PKFlash`, dashboards may show broken matches under the old unit and new matches under the corrected unit.
Local compiler data labels such as `@NNN` are preferable to invented global `it_803...` names when the data is not externally referenced.
Header declarations should only expose data that other files really need; this PR moved `it_803F6C18` into the C file and removed its old header extern.
When a header is renamed, update all character-side include users, not just the item files; Kirby copied-special files can be hidden consumers of Ness item APIs.
Scope annotations in `symbols.txt` matter for matching/linking, especially for static collision callbacks and local constants in `.data`, `.sdata`, `.sdata2`, and `.bss`.
naming_conventions: Use `itnesspkflash` and `itnesspkflashexplode` for Ness PK Flash item units; avoid the old incorrect `itnesspkflush` and `itnesspkflushexplode` names.
Main PK Flash callbacks in this PR use the lowercase-prefix style `itNesspkflash_*`, e.g. `itNesspkflash_UnkMotion0_Anim` and `itNesspkflash_Logic102_Reflected`.
PK Flash explosion callbacks use `itNessPKFlashExplode_*`, e.g. `itNessPKFlashExplode_UnkMotion0_Anim` and `itNessPKFlashExplode_Logic103_EvtUnk`.
Item motion callbacks continue the project pattern `UnkMotionN_Anim`, `UnkMotionN_Phys`, and `UnkMotionN_Coll`.
Item logic callbacks continue the `LogicNNN_EventName` pattern, with PK Flash using logic 102 and PK Flash Explode using logic 103.
The new `itnesspkflash.h` kept the address-based header guard `GALE01_2AA7E4`, matching the existing project style for some item headers.
Matched local data labels in symbols may be compiler-style `@NNN` and section labels like `...data.0`/`...bss.0`, not always semantic global names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Flip objects from `NonMatching` to `Matching` only after adding the missing data definitions that allow `.data` and item state tables to link.

Use exact `ItemStateTable` rows: PK Flash has states `0`, `1`, and `2`; Ness Bat has states `0` and `-1`; PK Fire, PK Thunder Ball, PK Thunder Trail, and PK Flash Explode each gained one-row state tables in this diff.
Represent internal collision callbacks as `static` in C and `scope:local` in `symbols.txt` when no external references require global symbols.
Keep split-unit names, source filenames, and symbol prefixes synchronized; otherwise decomp.dev may attribute matches to the wrong unit.
For data matching, replace guessed global data names with local `@NNN` labels and correct alignment/type metadata when the compiler would have emitted local constants.
No hand-written assembly changes are evident in this PR slice; the matching progress came from C source, data definitions, symbol metadata, and build configuration.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals are present in the captured slice.
The only captured comment is the decomp.dev bot report for GALE01: linked code 33.25% (+0.31%, +12020 bytes), matched data 37.68% (+0.03%, +336 bytes), and linked data 32.18% (+0.05%, +552 bytes).
The bot listed 41 new matches, including PK Flash motion/logic functions and `.data` for Ness PK Flash, PK Thunder Trail, PK Thunder Ball, PK Fire, Ness Bat, and PK Flash Explode.
The bot also listed 35 broken matches under the old `itnesspkflush`/`itnesspkflushexplode` unit names, consistent with the rename rather than an obvious functional regression.
follow_up_queries: Search for any remaining `pkflush` or `PKFlush` identifiers after PR 2299.
Audit other Ness item units for missing `ItemStateTable` definitions before marking them `Matching`.
Check whether `it_803F6C18` should remain private as `s32[]` or needs a correctly typed extern in any future header cleanup.
Review nearby item/effect symbol labels for invented globals that should be compiler-local `@NNN` labels.
When evaluating progress reports, distinguish true broken matches from unit renames that move matches from an old split name to a new split name.
